title: erysimum australe subsp. ruscinonense
intro: Erysimum australe subsp. ruscinonense, also known as a rare subspecies of the Austrian wallflower, is a flowering plant native to the Iberian Peninsula. Traditionally, the plant is used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects, which can be attributed to the presence of alkaloids and flavonoids. The health benefits of Erysimum australe subsp. ruscinonense include its ability to soothe digestive issues and relieve respiratory problems, making it a valuable remedy in folk medicine. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
